summary: Good‑quality audience tape of a fall‑'76 show, highlighted by an iconic Stella Blue and strong first‑set gems despite some source quirks.
review: The recording captures a solid late‑1976 performance with standout renditions of Stella Blue, Brown Eyed Women, Let It Grow, Tennessee Jed and the opening of Music Never Stopped. While the AUD source is uneven – missing the start of Promised Land, requiring slight pitch correction and showing an odd harmonizer effect on bass, drums and Jerry’s guitar – the band’s playing shines through, earning most reviewers a four‑star rating.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviews praise the musical performance and highlight several songs as exceptional; technical flaws are noted but do not dominate, leading to a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: AUD source is uneven – missing the start of Promised Land, requires +1‑2% pitch adjustment after Samson, balance is off and an added harmonizer/Octave divider introduces odd vocal/bass artifacts. The processing adds definition to bass/kick but can sound unnatural.

poor_songs: Stella Blue (as noted by one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely with strong mid‑set peaks; second‑set opener Music Never Stopped is flawless, but momentum wanes later with a weaker Friend of the Devil and dragged-out Sugar Mag.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is occasionally colored by the added harmonizer processing, though no specific performance complaints were noted.
band_member_comments.Bob: A vocal octave‑divider artifact was heard on his MC announcements, giving a ‘fat’ vocal effect.
band_member_comments.Drums: The harmonizer adds definition to the kick drum, but the effect can feel artificial at times.
